tasks.subject: Task 1: Fix stale theme allow-list in /api/preferences
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `backend/core/preferences.py`'s `THEMES` set includes all 14 current theme keys, so no theme silently resets to `rasputin-light` on save/load.

**Files:**
- Modify: `backend/core/preferences.py:13-23`
- Test: `tests/testBackendSmoke.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `THEMES` contains exactly the 14 keys from `frontend-src/src/lib/constants.js`'s `themeOptions`.
- [ ] Saving preferences with `theme: "cyberpunk-neon"` (or any of the other 4 previously-missing themes) round-trips unchanged through `_coerce()`.

**Verify:** `python -m pytest tests/testBackendSmoke.py -v -k theme` -> PASS

```json:metadata
{"files": ["backend/core/preferences.py", "tests/testBackendSmoke.py"], "verifyCommand": "python -m pytest tests/testBackendSmoke.py -v -k theme", "acceptanceCriteria": ["THEMES set contains all 14 theme keys", "POST /api/preferences with any of the 5 previously-missing themes round-trips unchanged"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 2: Remove redundant theme field from /api/settings general domain
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `/api/settings`'s `general` domain no longer carries a `theme` field, eliminating the second source of theme truth.

**Files:**
- Modify: `backend/core/settings_api.py:14-22`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `DEFAULT_SETTINGS["general"]` no longer has a `theme` key.
- [ ] `GET /api/settings` response's `general` object has no `theme` field.
- [ ] No other code in `settings_api.py` references `general.theme` (grep clean).

**Verify:** `grep -n "theme" backend/core/settings_api.py` -> no matches

```json:metadata
{"files": ["backend/core/settings_api.py"], "verifyCommand": "grep -n \"theme\" backend/core/settings_api.py", "acceptanceCriteria": ["DEFAULT_SETTINGS general domain has no theme key", "grep for theme in settings_api.py returns nothing"], "modelTier": "mechanical"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 3: Wire GeneralSettings theme dropdown to live theme/setTheme prop
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The Platform Theme dropdown in Settings always shows and controls the same theme as the rest of the app -- no separate save step, no drift after reload.

**Files:**
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/features/settings/GeneralSettings.jsx`
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/features/settings/SettingsView.jsx:23,102`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `GeneralSettings` receives `theme` as a prop (in addition to existing `setTheme`) and the `<select>`'s `value` is `theme`, not `formData.theme`.
- [ ] Selecting a new theme calls `setTheme(val)` only -- no `updateSetting("general", "theme", ...)` call, no entry in `formData`/`isDirty` for theme.
- [ ] After selecting a theme and reloading the page, the dropdown still shows the selected theme (verified in Task 10).

**Verify:** `grep -n "theme" frontend-src/src/features/settings/GeneralSettings.jsx` shows only `theme`/`setTheme` prop usage, no `formData.theme` or `handleChange("theme", ...)`.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["frontend-src/src/features/settings/GeneralSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/SettingsView.jsx"], "verifyCommand": "grep -n \"theme\" frontend-src/src/features/settings/GeneralSettings.jsx", "acceptanceCriteria": ["GeneralSettings select value bound to theme prop not formData.theme", "onChange calls setTheme directly with no updateSetting call for theme"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 4: Add Select and Switch UI primitives
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Two new token-driven primitives exist at `frontend-src/src/components/ui/select.jsx` and `frontend-src/src/components/ui/switch.jsx`, matching the existing thin-wrapper style of `input.jsx`/`button.jsx` (no new npm dependency).

**Files:**
- Create: `frontend-src/src/components/ui/select.jsx`
- Create: `frontend-src/src/components/ui/switch.jsx`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `Select` renders a native `<select>` styled with the same token classes as `Input`, forwards `ref`, accepts `children` and `className`.
- [ ] `Switch` renders a native `<input type="checkbox">` visually styled as a toggle using `--primary`/`--dash-*` tokens for the "on" state, accepts `checked`/`onChange`/`className`, forwards `ref`.

**Verify:** `npm run build` succeeds with no import errors.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["frontend-src/src/components/ui/select.jsx", "frontend-src/src/components/ui/switch.jsx"], "verifyCommand": "npm run build", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Select component exists and forwards ref", "Switch component exists and forwards ref", "npm run build succeeds"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 5: Migrate SettingsView shell and grouped collapsible nav onto dashboard tokens
tasks.description:
**Goal:** The Settings outer shell (header, section nav, inspector panel) uses `.tw`/`--dash-*`/shadcn tokens instead of react-bootstrap, and the flat 11-item nav is replaced with 4 collapsible groups (Platform/Security/Operations/System).

**Files:**
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/features/settings/SettingsView.jsx`
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/lib/constants.js`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `settingsGroups` export added to `constants.js`: Platform (general, models, integrations), Security (security, audit), Operations (runtime, deployments, resources, notifications), System (diagnostics, about).
- [ ] Each group header is clickable and toggles that group's expanded/collapsed state.
- [ ] No `react-bootstrap` imports remain in `SettingsView.jsx`.
- [ ] The inspector panel is visually restyled with token classes but `getInspectorText` content/logic is unchanged.
- [ ] Clicking a section still calls `setSection(id)` and navigates exactly as before.

**Verify:** `npm run build` -> succeeds; manual check in Task 10 confirms grouped nav renders and expands/collapses.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["frontend-src/src/features/settings/SettingsView.jsx", "frontend-src/src/lib/constants.js"], "verifyCommand": "npm run build", "acceptanceCriteria": ["settingsGroups exported from constants.js with 4 groups matching spec", "No react-bootstrap imports in SettingsView.jsx", "Group headers toggle collapse state", "Section click still navigates via setSection"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 6: Migrate GeneralSettings.jsx to dashboard tokens (migration template)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `GeneralSettings.jsx` is fully rewritten on `Card`/`UIButton`/`Select`/`Switch`/`Input` primitives -- this is the template the remaining 9 panels (Task 7) follow.

**Files:**
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/features/settings/GeneralSettings.jsx`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] No `react-bootstrap` imports remain.
- [ ] All visual elements render using token-driven primitives, consistent with dashboard styling (no white Bootstrap cards).
- [ ] The theme dropdown still reads/writes via `theme`/`setTheme` props per Task 3 (not reintroduced into `formData`).

**Verify:** `npm run build` succeeds; visual check in Task 10.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["frontend-src/src/features/settings/GeneralSettings.jsx"], "verifyCommand": "npm run build", "acceptanceCriteria": ["No react-bootstrap imports in GeneralSettings.jsx", "All controls use token-driven primitives", "Theme select still bound to theme/setTheme props"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 7: Migrate remaining 10 Settings panels to dashboard tokens
tasks.description:
**Goal:** `RuntimeSettings.jsx`, `SecuritySettings.jsx`, `ModelSettings.jsx`, `DeploymentSettings.jsx`, `IntegrationSettings.jsx`, `ResourceSettings.jsx`, `NotificationSettings.jsx`, `AuditSettings.jsx`, `DiagnosticsSettings.jsx`, and `AboutSettings.jsx` are all rewritten on the same primitives as `GeneralSettings.jsx`, with no react-bootstrap left anywhere in `frontend-src/src/features/settings/`.

**Files:**
- Modify each of the 10 listed settings panel files.

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `grep -rn "react-bootstrap" frontend-src/src/features/settings/` returns no matches.
- [ ] Every panel preserves its existing props, state shape, and `updateSetting(domain, key, value)` calls exactly as before.
- [ ] `ModelSettings.jsx` still receives and uses `models`, `modeModelOverrides`, `setModeModelOverride` exactly as before.
- [ ] `DiagnosticsSettings.jsx` and `AboutSettings.jsx` keep their existing state behavior -- only markup changes.

**Verify:** `grep -rn "react-bootstrap" frontend-src/src/features/settings/` -> no output. `npm run build` -> succeeds.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["frontend-src/src/features/settings/RuntimeSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/SecuritySettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/ModelSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/DeploymentSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/IntegrationSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/ResourceSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/NotificationSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/AuditSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/DiagnosticsSettings.jsx", "frontend-src/src/features/settings/AboutSettings.jsx"], "verifyCommand": "grep -rn \"react-bootstrap\" frontend-src/src/features/settings/", "acceptanceCriteria": ["No react-bootstrap imports anywhere in frontend-src/src/features/settings/", "All existing state/props/updateSetting calls preserved exactly", "npm run build succeeds"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 8: Add pinned field to sessions (backend) and pin-toggle action (frontend)
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Sessions support a `pinned` boolean end-to-end: schema migration, hub method, API route, and a frontend callback wired into `App.jsx`/`Sidebar.jsx`.

**Files:**
- Modify: `backend/core/runtime_store.py`
- Modify: `backend/engine/agent.py`
- Modify: `backend/api/agent.py`
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/app/App.jsx`
- Test: `tests/testBackendSmoke.py`

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `sessions` table has a `pinned INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0` column, added via the same migration-guard pattern used for `folder`.
- [ ] `POST /api/sessions/{id}/pinned` with `{"pinned": true}` sets the field and returns the updated session.
- [ ] `App.jsx` has a `pinSession(sessionId, pinned)` function calling that endpoint, refreshing sessions, and is passed to `<Sidebar>` via `sidebarProps`.

**Verify:** `python -m pytest tests/testBackendSmoke.py -v -k pinned` -> PASS

```json:metadata
{"files": ["backend/core/runtime_store.py", "backend/engine/agent.py", "backend/api/agent.py", "frontend-src/src/app/App.jsx", "tests/testBackendSmoke.py"], "verifyCommand": "python -m pytest tests/testBackendSmoke.py -v -k pinned", "acceptanceCriteria": ["sessions table has pinned column with migration guard", "POST /api/sessions/{id}/pinned sets and returns pinned state", "App.jsx pinSession function exists and is passed to Sidebar"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 9: Add pin-toggle button and Pinned/date grouping to Sidebar.jsx
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Recent Chats renders a Pinned group above date-based groups (Today/Yesterday/This Week/Older), and each chat row has a pin/unpin icon button.

**Files:**
- Modify: `frontend-src/src/components/Sidebar.jsx`
- Modify: relevant CSS file (wherever `.sidebar-session-folder-badge` is defined)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] `Sidebar` accepts a new `pinSession` prop.
- [ ] Each session row has a pin-toggle icon button calling `pinSession(session.id, !session.pinned)`.
- [ ] `visibleSessions` is grouped into Pinned / Today / Yesterday / This Week / Older; existing search/folder filtering and sort order still apply within each group.
- [ ] Empty groups are not rendered.
- [ ] The existing "no saved chats yet" empty state still shows when the fully-filtered list is empty.

**Verify:** `npm run build` succeeds; manual check confirms grouping and pin toggle in Task 10.

```json:metadata
{"files": ["frontend-src/src/components/Sidebar.jsx"], "verifyCommand": "npm run build", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Sidebar accepts pinSession prop", "Pin toggle button present per session row", "Sessions grouped into Pinned/Today/Yesterday/This Week/Older", "Empty groups not rendered", "Empty-state message still works when filtered list is empty"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```

tasks.subject: Task 10: End-to-end manual verification
tasks.description:
**Goal:** Confirm in a real running browser that the theme dropdown stays in sync after reload, all 14 themes visually apply to Settings, the grouped Settings nav works, and Recent Chats shows pin/date grouping correctly.

**USER-ORDERED GATE -- NON-SKIPPABLE.** This task was requested by the user in the current conversation. It MUST NOT be closed by walking around it, by declaring it "verified inline", or by substituting a cheaper check. Close only after every item in `acceptanceCriteria` has been re-validated independently, with output captured.

**Files:** none (verification only)

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] Switching the Platform Theme dropdown immediately re-themes both the dashboard/sidebar AND the Settings panel itself.
- [ ] After selecting a non-default theme and hard-reloading, the Settings dropdown shows the same theme that's visually applied.
- [ ] The Settings section nav shows 4 collapsible groups; group headers collapse/expand; sections navigate correctly.
- [ ] `grep -rn "react-bootstrap" frontend-src/src/features/settings/` returns no matches (re-run independently).
- [ ] Pinning a chat moves it into a "Pinned" group above date groups; unpinning moves it back.
- [ ] Date grouping renders without errors on existing session data.

**Verify:** Manual Playwright-driven walkthrough with screenshots captured at each acceptance criterion.

```json:metadata
{"userGate": true, "tags": ["user-gate"], "files": [], "verifyCommand": "grep -rn \"react-bootstrap\" frontend-src/src/features/settings/", "acceptanceCriteria": ["Theme dropdown stays in sync after reload", "Settings panel visually re-themes with all 14 themes, not just dashboard", "Settings nav shows 4 collapsible groups and navigates correctly", "No react-bootstrap imports remain in frontend-src/src/features/settings/", "Pinned chats appear in a Pinned group above date groups", "Date grouping renders without errors"], "modelTier": "standard"}
```
